step_by_step_thinking:
Clinical Reasoning:
While CT and MRI offer superior soft tissue and structural details critical for diagnosing complex spinal conditions like spondylolisthesis, standard X-rays — particularly the antero-posterior (AP) view — lack sufficient resolution to detect subtle slippage between vertebrae. Although lateral views show better alignment compared to AP views, they still cannot reliably confirm the extent or severity of spondylolisthesis due to limitations in depth perception and image quality. Among the given choices, only CT and MRI provide definitive confirmation of vertebral displacement, whereas X-ray AP views remain insufficient even though lateral views are somewhat helpful in initial assessments.
Answer choice: \boxed{C}
**Clinical Reasoning:**
CT and MRI provide high-resolution images necessary to accurately identify vertebral slippage in spondylolisthesis. While lateral X-rays can suggest possible slippage, the AP view lacks adequate anatomical detail and cannot reliably demonstrate displacement. Therefore, the AP view is the least useful for confirming diagnosis.
